title: labisia pumila var. alata
intro: Labisia pumila var. alata, also known as Kacip Fatimah, is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical forests of Malaysia and other parts of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Labisia pumila var. alata include improved fertility, reduced menstrual cramps, and relief from menopausal symptoms, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Labisia pumila var. alata is often prepared as an herbal tea, capsule, or tablet, and is also used in traditional Malay medicine to treat various health conditions.
